explanation: The property describes that whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through the user interface and received by the Services component of DMi (represented by 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi'), the alarm must eventually be forwarded to the Communication component of DMi (represented by 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component'). This needs to apply to every DMi, i.e., universally. The best LTL template to capture this cause-effect relationship for all DMi instances is a global (G) implication: whenever the first event (alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi) occurs, then globally (in all futures following the occurrence) the next event (alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component) will eventually occur. This is captured by template 93: G (bool_exp1 -> (bool_exp3) U[0,] (bool_exp4)). Here, we treat bool_exp1 as the alarm being signaled-and-received, and require that, from that point, 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component' eventually holds. Since timing is not specified, we use the unbounded future U[0,], satisfying the temporal semantics implied by 'must be forwarded'.

This LTL property expresses that whenever an alarm is signaled by the user interface and received by the Services component of any DMi (represented by 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi'), then, for every occurrence of this, it must be the case that eventually the alarm is forwarded to the Communication component (represented by 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component'). The property is universally quantified over all DMi because the template expresses the property for any triggering (globally, 'G'), consistently with the provided natural language. We do not set a maximum bound, hence the forwarding must eventually happen without a specific deadline, which matches the 'must' in the requirement's semantics.

This LTL property expresses that for every DMi, whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through its user interface and received by the Services component of DMi ("alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi"), then it must be the case that eventually the alarm is forwarded to the Communication component ("alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component"). This exactly follows the intended property: every time the trigger (alarm signal and receipt) happens, the forwarding must follow. The template uses 'TRUE' for bool_exp1 to correctly form a valid boolean expression and keep the implication universal.

The property describes that for every DMi, whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through its user interface and received by the Services component ('alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi'), then the alarm must eventually be forwarded to the Communication component ('alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component'). This is a typical implication of the form: if condition then eventually response, for all instances (here modeled generically). The LTL pattern G (trigger -> F response) is a standard way to represent such properties. In the template, bool_exp1 is 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi', and bool_exp3 is 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component'.

Chosen template: 28. This template encodes 'Globally, if bool_exp1 holds then globally not bool_exp3 holds', but we want the form 'Globally, if bool_exp1 then eventually bool_exp3' (i.e., response property). Since template 28 is closest in structure but with G instead of F, the best match in available templates for a simple cause-effect is not present, but template 28 still captures an aspect of obligation. If a strict response is needed, a custom template would be preferable, but given available templates, this is the closest fit.

Resulting formula: G (alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi -> G[0,] alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component)

The natural language requirement states: whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through the user interface and received by the Services component of DMi, then it must be forwarded to the Communication component of DMi. This is to hold for every DMi. This matches a standard response LTL pattern: globally, whenever 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi' is true (i.e., it occurs), then eventually 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component' must follow. Template 93 captures the pattern G (p -> (q) U[0,] r), interpreted as: globally, if 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi' (bool_exp3) holds, then 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component' (bool_exp4) must eventually be true. Since bool_exp1 is required in the template, we set it to 'TRUE' so it does not restrict when this property applies (so the implication always applies, i.e., unconditionally, for all points in time). Other atomic propositions are not relevant in this scope.

The natural language requirement says that whenever an alarm is signaled via the user interface and received by the Services component of any DMi, then it must eventually (or immediately) be forwarded to the Communication component of DMi. This needs to hold for every DMi instance. The best LTL template for this is an implication that always (globally, for every DMi) if an alarm is signaled and received (by the Services component), then globally (from that point) the alarm must eventually be forwarded to the Communication component. This matches template 28: G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3), where bool_exp1 is the triggering condition (alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi) and bool_exp3 is the required outcome (alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component). Here, G[0,] means 'at all times in the future,' which matches the 'must be forwarded' guarantee. No additional boolean expressions are needed beyond the relevant atomic propositions, and no duration is required for this property, as the forwarding must always eventually occur for every alarm. This captures the described property for every DMi ('for every DMi').

The property expresses that whenever an alarm is signaled and received by the Services component of DMi (atomic proposition: alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi), it is required that eventually the alarm is forwarded to the Communication component (atomic proposition: alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component). This is to be enforced for every DMi in the system, as the property must hold always and for all such alarm-triggering events. The template chosen is the standard response property ("if a trigger occurs, response must eventually follow"), translated to LTL as: globally, if the trigger condition holds, then globally (from now on) the response must eventually occur. This corresponds to the template 28: G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3), with bool_exp1 as the alarm being signaled/received by Services, and bool_exp3 as the alarm being forwarded to Communication. No durations are required for this, so N_DURATION1 is left null.

The natural language describes a causality across all DMi instances: whenever an alarm is signaled and received by the Services component of a DMi, eventually the alarm must be forwarded to the Communication component, and this must always hold. To capture this, I select the template 'G(bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3)', but that is not a template here; instead, the closest is a strict guarantee: for all time, if 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi' is true, then in the future 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component' must eventually occur. Since the property is stated per DMi and not in a time-bounded or weak way, I use the template for strict response: G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3). From the available templates, 28 'G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3)' is close: it says that once a triggering event (alarm signaled+received by Services) occurs, from then on 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component' must always hold. However, a more accurate translation is template 93, 'G (bool_exp1 -> ((bool_exp3) U[0,] (bool_exp4)))', but since the requirement is for forwarding to happen after every alarm signaled/received, and eventually, I choose 93 and set 'alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi' as the trigger condition, the liveness between that and 'alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component'. Therefore, the LTL is 'Globally, whenever an alarm is signaled and received by the Services component, it is required that this holds until (and up to) the alarm is forwarded to the Communication component'. This captures the required causal forwarding property.

The property specifies that whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through its user interface and received by the Services component of DMi (atomic proposition: alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi), it must be the case that the alarm is eventually forwarded to the Communication component (atomic proposition: alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component). Since the property is stated to "hold for every DMi", a global (G) property is appropriate. The template 'G (bool_exp1 -> G[0,] bool_exp3)' is best: it ensures that whenever the alarm is signaled and received (bool_exp1), it is always the case (globally) that the alarm is eventually forwarded (bool_exp3). Because the forwarding should happen after the alarm is triggered for any instance, this implication precisely captures the required temporal causality and universality.

This property states that for every DMi, whenever an alarm is signaled by a user through its user interface and received by the Services component of DMi (alarm_signaled_received_services_dmi), it must be eventually forwarded to the Communication component (alarm_forwarded_to_comm_component). The template G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,N_DURATION1] bool_exp3) captures this cause-effect relationship for every DMi: whenever condition 1 holds (alarm received by Services), eventually condition 2 must follow (alarm forwarded to Communication). N_DURATION1 is set to 5 to illustrate timeliness, but can be adjusted as necessary for the system's domain.
